review_text: after a while, you have to buy a premium subscription so you can go back to a song, and you also can't replay the song after a while it's just really annoying so I really hope this can be fixed but if it doesn't then I will most likely delete the app. Even though I have worked on it very hard... But also I'll be playing a game like Dandys World or smth, and i will get silence from the app and i will be listening to a song, and then it will just stop randomly playing the song! So please fix this.
summary: The user is frustrated by limitations of the free tier, such as inability to go back or replay songs, and also experiences random song stoppages.
pain_points: cannot go back to a song on free tier
cannot replay songs on free tier
feature_requests: allow going back to songs on free tier
allow replaying songs on free tier
user_goal: Listen to music without interruptions and with basic playback controls.
root_cause: Free tier feature restrictions; app performance bug causing random stoppages.

review_text: To say that this app is greedy would be an understatement. You can't even choose the songs you want to listen to, ads are everywhere and it will continue to shove it into your face. You can't skip, you can't make a playlist where it's not on shuffle, and it's genuinely the epitome of a prison if it was an app and if it arrested you for not being on premium. I would rate this 0 star if I could.
summary: The user is extremely angry about the restrictive free tier experience, including inability to choose songs, excessive ads, no skipping, and forced shuffle on playlists.
pain_points: cannot choose specific songs on free tier
cannot skip songs on free tier
forced shuffle on playlists on free tier
feature_requests: allow choosing specific songs on free tier
allow skipping songs on free tier
allow non-shuffle playlists on free tier
user_goal: Listen to music with full control over playback and without interruptions.
root_cause: Aggressive free tier limitations designed to push users to premium.

review_text: Super interesting that Spotify said in-app that they were asking for "bluetooth" permission when what was REALLY requested was permission to locate/connect with nearby devices. (I was listening to music using bluetooth while this was happening, so it wasn't because my BT was off.) Why do you want that, Spotify? Couldn't be anything good if you have to trick people into granting you access. I'm switching to Tidal. So tired of this bull.
summary: The user is angry about Spotify misleadingly requesting "bluetooth" permission when it actually sought permission to locate/connect with nearby devices, leading them to switch to a competitor.
pain_points: misleading permission requests (bluetooth vs. nearby devices)
lack of transparency regarding data access
user_goal: Use Spotify without feeling deceived about privacy or data access.

review_text: Great way to listen to music and affordable pricing for premium subscription. Edit: Everything in this app is good and conveniently adjustable to one's needs, except the damned "smart shuffle". The radio is more than enough to discover new songs. When I want to shuffle/unshuffle my own playlists I could not give half a damn about discovering new songs. Please give us the option to turn it off or on in settings, the same way you can toggle all the other features you provide.
summary: The user praises Spotify's overall value and customizability but is frustrated by the "Smart Shuffle" feature, which forces new song discovery when they only want to shuffle their own playlists, and requests an option to disable it.
pain_points: Smart Shuffle forces new song discovery
Smart Shuffle interferes with desired playlist shuffling
feature_requests: add option to turn off Smart Shuffle in settings
positive_themes: great way to listen to music
app is good and conveniently adjustable
user_goal: Listen to music from their own playlists with simple shuffle functionality, and discover new music via Radio.
root_cause: Smart Shuffle's default behavior or lack of user control over its activation.
discovery_issue: Smart Shuffle forces new song discovery when the user only wants to shuffle their own playlists.

review_text: For the last few months I haven't been able to play liked songs for a majority of the time. I can play them by searching for them or playing from a specific playlist but not being able to listen to the liked songs playlist is very frustrating and defeats the whole purpose of the app. Occasionally it will work for a few days then stop again. I've deleted and reinstalled the app and it doesn't fix anything.
summary: The user is frustrated because they are frequently unable to play songs from their "Liked Songs" playlist, despite being able to play the same songs via search or other playlists.
pain_points: unable to play songs from Liked Songs playlist
Liked Songs playlist functionality is intermittent
feature_requests: fix Liked Songs playlist playback issue
user_goal: Consistently play music from their Liked Songs playlist.
root_cause: Bug in the Liked Songs playlist playback functionality.

review_text: Generally I love Spotify over most other streaming apps. I just started using it for audio books as well since some are included with premium. It's absolutely wild that you "run out of hours" to listen to a book, especially when those limited hours are less than it takes to get through a book. Why offer audiobooks with premium at all if you're going to charge more to keep listening to a book that's "included" with premium?
summary: The user generally loves Spotify but is disappointed by the limited audiobook hours included with premium, which often run out before a book is finished, questioning the value proposition.
pain_points: audiobook hours run out before book completion
additional charges for continued audiobook listening
feature_requests: increase audiobook hours included with premium
remove additional charges for audiobooks included with premium
positive_themes: loves Spotify over other streaming apps
user_goal: Listen to full audiobooks as part of their premium subscription.
root_cause: Premium tier limitations on audiobook listening hours.

review_text: "Enjoy the next 30 minutes commercial free." 1 song plays, then 3 commercials, literally 4 minutes after the message. Lies. Another thing? I thumb down an ad, because I'm married 20 years and don't use dating apps, or no kids and every third ad is for Capri Sun or school supplies. Your targeted ads suck. The feedback form doesn't fix it; thumb down/does not apply/etc: it doesn't work. 1/3 off my ads are for Kamala, and every one pushes me farther to the right.
summary: The user is angry about misleading "commercial free" promises, irrelevant and poorly targeted ads, and the ineffectiveness of ad feedback mechanisms.
user_goal: Listen to music with minimal and relevant ad interruptions, or without ads as promised.
root_cause: Inaccurate ad scheduling/promises; ineffective ad targeting algorithm; broken ad feedback system.

review_text: used to be better. you have to pay for basic features now. you can't make a playlist without it adding unwanted extra songs. I hate it so much. when I want to play a song, it starts playing suggested songs instead of the thing I want it to play. awful. still decent app, though at this rate, youtube is better.
summary: The user is frustrated that basic features now require payment and that the app automatically adds unwanted songs to playlists and plays suggested songs instead of the user's chosen track.
pain_points: unwanted extra songs added to playlists
app plays suggested songs instead of chosen track
feature_requests: stop automatically adding unwanted songs to playlists
user_goal: Create and play playlists with full control over song selection, without forced discovery or premium restrictions.
root_cause: Free tier restrictions and aggressive recommendation/playlist enhancement features.
discovery_issue: The app automatically adds unwanted songs to playlists and plays suggested songs instead of the user's chosen track.

review_text: Doesn't play well with Google Pixel 8. Stops connecting to my cars Bluetooth until I restart my phone (won't play any audio). Music stutters while playing. "Upstream request" timeouts when I try to access their website. Player won't work on desktop. Wrapped won't play. I restart the app. I install updates. I check settings. I try different browsers. Terrible user experience with Spotify this year. [Edit] I appreciate the response. I have tried reinstalling. I can't reach your site, but will try
summary: The user reports numerous performance and connectivity issues, including problems with Bluetooth on Google Pixel 8, music stuttering, website access failures, desktop player malfunction, and Wrapped not playing.
user_goal: Consistently play music across devices and access Spotify's features without technical issues.
root_cause: Multiple software bugs and compatibility issues across different platforms and devices.

review_text: I used to really like Spotify because it was extremely simple and had a large library. It hasn't changed too much, but the problem is that over the years this app has started to work less and less. In the modern day Spotify feels barely functional: Constantly crashing, extremely slow, skipping large portions of playlists, a broken shuffle feature, and buggy bluetooth drivers. I just need an app that plays music and actually works.
summary: The user, who previously liked Spotify for its simplicity and large library, is now disappointed by its poor performance, including constant crashing, slowness, playlist skipping, broken shuffle, and buggy Bluetooth.
user_goal: Use a functional music app that reliably plays music.
root_cause: Multiple software bugs leading to instability and poor performance.
